cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The question is asking when an ordinary tropical storm is called a hurricane. The options are A through D. First, I'll need to refer back to the passage provided. Let me scan through the first paragraph again. The first sentence says, "In the second half of each year, many powerful storms are born in the tropical Atlantic and Caribbean seas. Of these, only about half a dozen becomes the strong, circling winds of 75 miles per hour or more that are called hurricanes..." So here, it mentions that storms become hurricanes when they have winds of 75 mph or more. Looking at the answer choices, option D is "When its winds reach 75 miles per hour." That seems to directly match what the passage states. Let me check the other options to make sure. Option A says when it begins in the Atlantic and Caribbean. The passage does mention that storms are born there, but not all of them become hurricanes. Only those with 75 mph winds. So A is not correct. Option B is when it hits the coastline.
